note: Curated shortlist of biblical NARRATIVES for 'Your story' generation. Same anti-hallucination pattern as verses.json: we store references, themes, and a neutral synopsis — never generated 'scripture'. The story engine weaves the user's moment into ONE of these vetted narratives; any verse text shown verbatim is fetched from YouVersion. Synopses are descriptive summaries (our own words), reviewed for fidelity.

stories.title: Elijah under the broom tree
stories.synopsis: After his greatest victory, Elijah collapses in the wilderness, spent and wanting out. God's answer isn't a lecture — it's sleep, bread, and a gentle voice, twice, before any next step is asked of him.

stories.title: The sower and the soils
stories.synopsis: A farmer scatters seed everywhere — path, rocks, thorns, good soil — and most of it seems wasted. The harvest comes anyway, out of proportion to the loss, from the seed that lands and stays.

stories.title: The prodigal son
stories.synopsis: A son burns every bridge, rehearses a servant's apology on the road home — and the father is running toward him before a word is out, restoring the sonship the boy thought he'd spent.

stories.title: Peter walks on the water
stories.synopsis: In a night storm Peter steps out of the boat and walks — until he looks at the wind instead of the face in front of him. He sinks one arm's length from rescue; the hand is already there.

stories.title: The widow's oil
stories.synopsis: A widow drowning in debt has one small jar of oil. She's told to borrow empty vessels — as many as she dares — and the oil keeps flowing exactly as long as there is something to fill.

stories.title: Hagar at the well
stories.synopsis: A runaway servant, pregnant and disposable in everyone else's story, is found by a spring in the desert. She gives God the name no one else uses: the One who sees me.

stories.title: Hannah's prayer
stories.synopsis: Hannah prays so raw and wordless that the priest thinks she's drunk. She isn't answered that day — but she walks home lighter, having poured the whole weight of it out.

stories.title: David and Goliath
stories.synopsis: The armor they offer him doesn't fit, so he leaves it. Five smooth stones and a history of rescued sheep — David fights the giant as exactly who he already is, not who they told him to be.

stories.title: Job in the ashes
stories.synopsis: Job loses everything in a single afternoon and refuses easy answers — his own and his friends'. The book lets him rage and grieve for thirty chapters before any voice answers from the storm.

stories.title: Ruth stays
stories.synopsis: Two widows on an empty road. Naomi has nothing left to offer, and Ruth binds herself to her anyway — where you go, I go. Loyalty, not optimism, is what carries them home.

stories.title: Jesus calms the storm
stories.synopsis: The boat is filling and he's asleep on a cushion — which feels like abandonment until he stands up. The storm obeys a sentence. The disciples' fear changes object, not size.

stories.title: Feeding the five thousand
stories.synopsis: The math is hopeless — five loaves, two fish, thousands hungry. The boy hands over his lunch anyway, and the impossibility is fed with twelve baskets left over.

stories.title: The lost sheep
stories.synopsis: Ninety-nine are safe, and the shepherd goes out into the dark for the one — not because the one earned it, but because that's what this shepherd is like. He carries it home on his shoulders.

stories.title: Mary and Martha
stories.synopsis: Martha is doing everything right and drowning in it; Mary just sits down at his feet. The rebuke is gentle: you are anxious about many things, and only one is needed.

stories.title: Songs at midnight
stories.synopsis: Beaten and chained in the innermost cell, Paul and Silas sing at midnight — before the earthquake, not after. The jailer meets a freedom that was in the room before the doors opened.

stories.title: Thomas and the scars
stories.synopsis: Thomas won't pretend a faith he doesn't have, and Jesus doesn't shame him for it — he shows up a week later with his scars out, and offers the exact evidence Thomas asked for.

stories.title: The woman at the well
stories.synopsis: She comes at noon to avoid the crowd and their opinions. The stranger at the well knows her whole story and stays in the conversation anyway — and she runs back to the very town she was hiding from.

stories.title: Zacchaeus in the tree
stories.synopsis: A rich man everyone despises climbs a tree like a child just to see. Out of the whole crowd, Jesus stops at his branch and invites himself to dinner — and the man climbs down changed.

stories.title: Joseph — meant for good
stories.synopsis: Sold by his brothers, slandered, forgotten in prison — and years later Joseph stands in front of them with power to repay it all. What you meant for evil, God wove toward good.

stories.title: Moses at the burning bush
stories.synopsis: Moses has five objections, starting with 'who am I?' The voice in the fire never argues his adequacy — it answers every excuse with a presence: I will be with you.

stories.title: Jonah and the second word
stories.synopsis: Jonah runs the exact opposite direction and sinks all the way down. The tide turns on eight words: the word of the LORD came a second time. Failure didn't disqualify him.

stories.title: Gideon, mighty man of valor
stories.synopsis: Gideon is threshing wheat in a winepress, hiding from the enemy, when the angel greets him: mighty man of valor. God names the courage before Gideon has shown any.

stories.title: Blind Bartimaeus
stories.synopsis: The crowd tells him to be quiet; he shouts louder. Jesus stops the whole procession for a beggar's voice and asks the dignifying question: what do you want me to do for you?

stories.title: The road to Emmaus
stories.synopsis: Two disciples walk away from the wreckage of everything they hoped, and a stranger falls in step and lets them say all of it. Their hearts burn long before their eyes open at the table.

stories.title: Gethsemane
stories.synopsis: Jesus himself is overwhelmed to the point of death and prays the same anguished sentence three times, sweat like blood. Honest dread and full surrender live in the same prayer.

stories.title: Jesus wept
stories.synopsis: He knows resurrection is four minutes away — and he still stands at his friend's tomb and weeps with the sisters first. Grief is not a lack of faith; he honored it before he answered it.

stories.title: The fourth man in the fire
stories.synopsis: Three men refuse to bow, saying 'our God is able — but even if not…' They aren't spared the furnace; they're met inside it, and walk out without the smell of smoke.

stories.title: Simeon and Anna wait
stories.synopsis: Two old believers have waited their entire lives on a promise, showing up to the same temple, decade after decade. One ordinary morning, the waiting ends in their arms.

stories.title: The seventh day
stories.synopsis: The first thing ever called holy is not a mountain or a temple — it's a day off. Rest is stitched into the fabric of creation before anyone had earned it.

stories.title: Why so downcast, my soul
stories.synopsis: The psalmist argues with his own soul in the dark — why are you cast down? — and preaches hope to himself twice, not because the feelings resolved, but because the address still stands.
